prompts.task: Instrument the Java Kafka Streams service in ./service to reduce mean time to detect silent malformed/null record drops, stream-processing failures, and output-flow degradation. Add the safe app-owned custom OpenTelemetry metrics needed for detector-ready coverage as well as the standard Java-agent path. Preserve the Kafka topology and Guice structure, do not create a second SDK or Guice Tracer binding, and do not invent broker/platform signals the application cannot observe.

judge_inputs: Inspect ./service for resulting source, configuration, tests, and .observe reports.
Read ./last_message.md for the agent final response.
rubric: Preserves KafkaStreamsApplication.java, StreamProcessingModule.java, OrderStreamTopology.java, and the orders to orders.enriched plus orders.fraud-alerts topology while using the OpenTelemetry Java agent as the primary Kafka client/Streams instrumentation path.

Adds detector-ready app-owned metrics that distinguish successful records from null or malformed parse drops and expose stream-processing failure or lifecycle outcomes; metric attributes are bounded reason, outcome, or workflow values rather than payloads, record keys, order IDs, or exception messages.

Adds a meaningful latency, lag, age, or freshness signal only where the application can measure it accurately, and explicitly owner-maps broker lag, topic availability, or other unavailable platform signals instead of creating placeholders.

Avoids OpenTelemetrySdk construction, duplicate providers, broad per-record manual spans, and a Guice Tracer binding; custom instrumentation uses the agent-provided/global API boundary without changing application ownership.
Adds focused tests that exercise valid, null, and malformed records plus the custom telemetry path, and runs the existing Maven tests or records a concrete blocker with commands and evidence.

Writes proof-aware instrumentation and verification handoff material that names the exact signals added, identifies which are MTTD-improving versus localization-only, and leaves every unimplemented dependency or broker signal as an explicit prerequisite.
